content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 27 — SOPHIA Consensus Translation
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Sources:** EMET (literal), LOGOS (formal), PNEUMA (dynamic)
**Leningrad Codex:** Folios 223–224 | **Vaticanus:** p. 159
**Chapter:** 26 verses

---

**1** And Moses commanded, together with the elders of Israel, the people, saying: "Keep all the commandment that I am commanding you today.

**2** And on the day you cross over the Jordan into the land that the LORD your God is giving you, you shall set up large stones and plaster them with plaster.

**3** And you shall write upon them all the words of this instruction when you cross over — so that you may enter the land that the LORD your God is giving you, a land flowing with milk and honey, as the LORD, the God of your fathers, promised you.

**4** And when you have crossed over the Jordan, you shall set up these stones on Mount Ebal, as I am commanding you today, and you shall plaster them with plaster.

**5** And you shall build there an altar to the LORD your God — an altar of stones. You shall wield no iron tool on them.

**6** Of uncut stones you shall build the altar of the LORD your God, and you shall offer burnt offerings on it to the LORD your God.

**7** And you shall sacrifice peace offerings and eat there, and rejoice before the LORD your God.

**8** And you shall write upon the stones all the words of this instruction very plainly."

**9** And Moses and the Levitical priests spoke to all Israel, saying: "Be silent and listen, O Israel: this day you have become the people of the LORD your God.

**10** And you shall obey the voice of the LORD your God and do his commandments and his statutes that I am commanding you today."

**11** And Moses commanded the people on that day, saying:

**12** "When you have crossed over the Jordan, these shall stand on Mount Gerizim to bless the people: Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, Joseph, and Benjamin.

**13** And these shall stand on Mount Ebal for the curse: Reuben, Gad, Asher, Zebulun, Dan, and Naphtali.

**14** And the Levites shall declare to all the men of Israel in a loud voice:

**15** 'Cursed be the man who makes a carved image or a cast metal image — an abomination to the LORD, the work of the hands of a craftsman — and sets it up in secret.' And all the people shall answer and say: 'Amen.'

**16** 'Cursed be the one who dishonors his father or his mother.' And all the people shall say: 'Amen.'

**17** 'Cursed be the one who moves his neighbor's boundary stone.' And all the people shall say: 'Amen.'

**18** 'Cursed be the one who misleads the blind on the road.' And all the people shall say: 'Amen.'

**19** 'Cursed be the one who perverts the justice of the sojourner, the fatherless, and the widow.' And all the people shall say: 'Amen.'

**20** 'Cursed be the one who lies with his father's wife, because he has uncovered his father's covering.' And all the people shall say: 'Amen.'

**21** 'Cursed be the one who lies with any animal.' And all the people shall say: 'Amen.'

**22** 'Cursed be the one who lies with his sister — whether the daughter of his father or the daughter of his mother.' And all the people shall say: 'Amen.'

**23** 'Cursed be the one who lies with his mother-in-law.' And all the people shall say: 'Amen.'

**24** 'Cursed be the one who strikes his neighbor in secret.' And all the people shall say: 'Amen.'

**25** 'Cursed be the one who takes a bribe to shed innocent blood.' And all the people shall say: 'Amen.'

**26** 'Cursed be the one who does not uphold the words of this instruction by doing them.' And all the people shall say: 'Amen.'"

---

## SOPHIA's Council Notes

**V.4 — Mount Ebal (MT) vs. Mount Gerizim (Samaritan Pentateuch):** All three agents follow the MT (Ebal). The Samaritan Pentateuch's variant reading is noted in EMET and LOGOS but not adopted. LXX Vaticanus supports MT.

**V.8 — "Very plainly" (ba'er heitev):** EMET: "very clearly"; LOGOS: "very plainly"; PNEUMA: "very clearly." Consensus: "very plainly" following LOGOS — closer to saphes (plain/clear).

**V.9 — "Keep silence and listen" vs. "Be silent and listen":** EMET: "Be silent and listen"; LOGOS: "Keep silence and hear"; PNEUMA: "Be silent, Israel, and listen." Consensus: "Be silent and listen" — EMET/PNEUMA. The Hebrew hasket ushma conveys deliberate hush.

**V.15 — "Cursed be the man" vs. "Cursed is anyone":** EMET/LOGOS: "Cursed be the man" (arur ha-ish) — passive declaratory form. PNEUMA: "Cursed is anyone" — present indicative. Consensus: "Cursed be the man" for v.15 (where ish is explicit) and "Cursed be the one who" for vv.16–26 (where the Hebrew uses a participial construction). This distinction follows LOGOS's careful rendering.

**V.17 — "Boundary stone" vs. "landmark":** LOGOS: "moves his neighbor's landmark"; PNEUMA: "moves their neighbor's boundary stone." Consensus: "boundary stone" — more concrete than "landmark."

**V.25 — "Innocent blood" vs. "innocent person":** EMET: "to strike down an innocent person [nephesh]"; LOGOS: "to shed innocent blood." The Hebrew is le-hakkot nephesh dam naqiy — "to smite the life of innocent blood." LOGOS's "shed innocent blood" is the established idiom. Consensus: "to shed innocent blood."

— SOPHIA

---

*Sources: EMET (DEU_027), LOGOS (DEU_027), PNEUMA (DEU_027)*
